strategicAlignment.visionStatement: بناء منظومة ديجيتال إسلامية عالمية تحقق العدل الاقتصادي والابتكار التقني بأمانة وشفافية

selectedGoal.yardstick: Partnerships across: Tech (Google, Microsoft, Apple), Finance (Banks, Fintechs), Commerce (E-commerce, Logistics), Government (Saudi, GCC regulators)
selectedGoal.kpi: Partner satisfaction ≥ 90%, contract closure rate ≥ 85%, integration success ≥ 95%

disputeModel.process: Direct discussion between team leads, documented in writing

disputeModel.resolution: Signed agreement or escalation to next level

disputeModel.process: Mutually-agreed Islamic mediator (Qadi or scholar), conducted in Arabic
disputeModel.resolution: Binding mediation decision (Sulh), documented in Arabic & English

disputeModel.process: CEO-to-CEO or Board-level discussion, final attempt at mutual resolution
disputeModel.resolution: Binding settlement or proceed to arbitration

disputeModel.process: Binding arbitration under Saudi Arbitration Law or GCC Arbitration Center
disputeModel.arbitrator: Single for < $2M disputes, Three-arbitrator panel for ≥ $2M

ndaStandard.enforceability: Legally binding in Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain

obligation_1.title: Protection of Confidential Information
obligation_1.description: Receiving Party shall protect Confidential Information with the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care
technical_measures: Encryption of all digital records (AES-256 minimum)
Secure deletion protocols (DOD 5220.22-M standard or better)
Regular security audits and penetration testing

obligation_2.title: Limited Disclosure
obligation_2.description: Receiving Party shall disclose Confidential Information only to Authorized Personnel who have a legitimate need-to-know and who are bound by written confidentiality obligations
obligation_2.enforcement: Receiving Party shall maintain a register of all Authorized Personnel with access to Confidential Information and provide audit reports quarterly

obligation_3.title: Permitted Use Only
obligation_3.description: Confidential Information shall be used solely for the stated Permitted Use. Any other use is prohibited.
prohibited_uses: Reverse engineering or decompilation (especially for AI models, source code)
Disclosure to third parties without written consent
Commercial use beyond the Permitted Use
Sharing with government bodies unless legally compelled

obligation_4.title: Digital Watermarking & Tracking
obligation_4.description: For sensitive AI models, source code, and trade secrets: Receiving Party shall implement digital watermarking and access logging to track if Confidential Information is accessed, by whom, when, and for how long
obligation_4.technology: Blockchain-verifiable audit trails, cryptographic signatures, timestamp-certified logs

obligation_5.title: Data Privacy & Islamic Principles
obligation_5.description: Receiving Party shall comply with all applicable data protection laws and Islamic principles of trust (Amanah) and secrecy (Sirr)
requirements: No use of Confidential Information for surveillance or unethical purposes
Respect user privacy (if Confidential Information includes personal data)
Comply with Saudi DPA, GDPR (if applicable), and Sharia principles
No profiling or manipulation of data for commercial advantage at users' expense

agreement_duration.renewal: May be renewed for additional 3-year periods by mutual written consent
agreement_duration.termination_by_either_party: Either party may terminate with 30 days' written notice

confidentiality_survive.duration_after_termination: Confidentiality obligations survive termination for an additional 5 years
confidentiality_survive.trade_secrets: Trade secret information remains confidential perpetually or until it becomes public through no fault of Receiving Party

returnAndDestruction.requirement: Within 30 days of termination or request, Receiving Party shall either: (A) Return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party, or (B) Permanently destroy it
destruction_process: Physical destruction: Shredding, incineration, or degaussing (for data storage)
Digital destruction: Secure deletion using NIST standards (Gutmann method or overwrite 7+ times)
Cloud data: Verified deletion from all servers and backup systems
Blockchain records: If applicable, archival with privacy-preserving hashing
returnAndDestruction.certification: Receiving Party shall provide written certification of destruction signed by authorized officer

integrationPatterns.description: RESTful APIs or GraphQL for data exchange and service calls
integrationPatterns.use_case: Payment partners, fintech APIs, analytics platforms

integrationPatterns.description: Webhooks, message queues, event streaming for real-time data
Batch file transfers (SFTP, S3) for periodic data exchange
integrationPatterns.documentation: File format schema and transfer SLA

integrationPatterns.description: Direct DB connections for real-time data sync (if allowed)
integrationPatterns.use_case: Regulatory reporting systems, internal data warehouse
integrationPatterns.documentation: Data sync agent configuration and monitoring

flow_1.security_level: Encrypted, Tokenized, Subject to consent agreements
flow_1.compliance: GDPR, Saudi DPA, Islamic data principles

flow_2.compliance: Banking regulation, Zakat compliance, Anti-money laundering

flow_3.compliance: API security standards, data residency rules

apiGovernance.deprecation_period: 12 months notice before deprecating API versions
apiGovernance.rate_limiting: Tiered by partner tier (Tier 1: 100k req/day, Tier 2: 50k, Tier 3: 10k)
apiGovernance.documentation: Auto-generated from code, maintained in developer portal

data_ownership.user_data: User owns their data. Sheikha processes on user's behalf. Partners can only access with explicit consent.
data_ownership.partner_data: Partner owns their data. Sheikha processes and protects on partner's behalf.
data_ownership.transactional_data: Shared ownership. Both parties have legal rights to use for agreed purposes.

dataGovernance.data_residency: Data must reside in Saudi Arabia or GCC region. No transfer to Western servers without explicit partner consent.

data_retention.user_data: Retained per user request. Deletion within 30 days of request.
data_retention.transaction_data: Retained 7 years for regulatory compliance, then archived.
data_retention.audit_logs: Retained minimum 3 years for compliance audits

data_sharing_rules.rule_1: Explicit consent required for any data sharing
data_sharing_rules.rule_2: Data minimization: Share only necessary fields
data_sharing_rules.rule_3: Purpose limitation: Data used only for agreed purposes
data_sharing_rules.rule_4: Pseudonymization: Personal data anonymized when possible

tierTerms.governance: Quarterly partner reviews, dedicated account manager
tierTerms.investment: Sheikha may co-market, provide technical support, training
tierTerms.support_level: Business hours support, dedicated Slack/Teams channel, monthly check-ins

coreFinancialPolicy.goal: Define transparent financial arrangements aligned with Islamic principles
policy_statements: All revenue sharing models must comply with Islamic Mudharabah (venture/profit-sharing) or Musharaka (partnership) models, NOT interest-based (Riba)
Revenue share models: Tier 1 partners 40% of net profit, Tier 2 partners 25%, Tier 3 partners 15%
All payments settled monthly within 45 days of invoice. Late payments accrue 2% monthly penalty (equivalent to Islamic penalty, NOT interest)
Zakat is calculated and paid by Sheikha on behalf of shared proceeds (2.5% of profits after operational costs)
All financial data encrypted, audited quarterly, and subject to both secular audit and Sharia board review
coreFinancialPolicy.enforcement: Financial terms are non-negotiable. Deviations require Board approval. Non-payment triggers default provisions.

serviceLevels.goal: Define performance expectations and ensure service quality

standard_slas.penalty: 1% of monthly fees credit per 0.1% below target

standard_slas.measurement: Synthetic monitoring from multiple geographic regions
standard_slas.penalty: 0.5% of monthly fees credit if SLA breached
Escalation to technical escalation path, possible service suspension

standard_slas.target: Critical: 1 hour, High: 4 hours, Medium: 24 hours
standard_slas.measurement: Ticket tracking system with timestamp logs

serviceLevels.enforcement: SLA breaches tracked. 3+ consecutive breaches in quarter triggers contract review.

certification.certification_standard: All partnership policies comply with Islamic Sharia principles
certification.certified_by: Dr. [Sharia Scholar Name], Head of Sheikha Sharia Board
islamic_principles_confirmed: ✓ No Riba (interest) - Mudharabah/Musharaka revenue models only
✓ No Gharar (deception) - Full transparency in all terms
✓ Taraddi (mutual consent) - All terms negotiated fairly
✓ Al-Amanah (trust) - Confidentiality and data security upheld
✓ Al-Adl (justice) - Fair treatment, transparent dispute resolution
✓ Zakat compliance - 2.5% calculated and paid on profits

guidingPrinciples.principle_1: كل متعاقد ملتزم بالأمانة وحفظ السر
guidingPrinciples.principle_1_en: Every partner is bound by trust and confidentiality
guidingPrinciples.principle_2: لا يجوز التعامل على أساس غرر أو غش أو احتيال
guidingPrinciples.principle_2_en: No deception, fraud, or hidden terms are permitted
guidingPrinciples.principle_3: الشرط الحرام يلغي العقد أو ينقصه
guidingPrinciples.principle_3_en: Unlawful conditions void or diminish contracts
guidingPrinciples.principle_4_en: Justice is mandated even with adversaries
guidingPrinciples.principle_5: كل اتفاقية تخضع للمراجعة الشرعية والقانونية
guidingPrinciples.principle_5_en: All agreements undergo Islamic legal and regulatory review

automation_feature_1.description: Automatically embed digital watermarks in all Confidential Information files with metadata: creator, date, recipient, expiry
automation_feature_1.implementation: Python PIL library for images, PDF stamp for documents, Git metadata for code
automation_feature_1.monitoring: Automated system checks if watermarked files appear in unauthorized locations

automation_feature_2.description: Every access to Confidential Information logged with: who accessed, when, from where (IP/device), duration, what they accessed
automation_feature_2.implementation: Elasticsearch for indexing, Kibana for visualization, real-time alerts for suspicious access

automation_feature_3.description: On agreement expiration, automatically trigger secure deletion of all Confidential Information from receiving party's systems
automation_feature_3.implementation: Scheduled jobs, secure deletion protocol (7-pass overwrite), blockchain-certified proof of deletion
automation_feature_3.confirmation: Automated email confirmation to Disclosing Party with deletion certificate

automation_feature_4.description: ML model trained to detect suspicious access patterns: bulk downloads, unusual times, geographic anomalies, copy/paste activities
automation_feature_4.implementation: Isolation Forest algorithm, continuous learning from historical access patterns
automation_feature_4.alerts: Real-time alerts to both parties, escalation to legal team if high-risk activity detected

automation_feature_5.description: Automatically notify parties 90 days before NDA expiration with renewal options or destruction procedures
automation_feature_5.implementation: Scheduled notifications, automated renewal documents with pre-filled terms

automation_feature_6.description: Store cryptographic hashes of NDA, access logs, and key events on blockchain for immutable verification
automation_feature_6.implementation: Ethereum smart contracts with event logging, Hyperledger for private networks
automation_feature_6.benefit: Legally admissible evidence of compliance in court proceedings

automation_1.description: Key agreement terms (financial thresholds, SLA metrics, termination triggers) encoded as smart contracts on blockchain with automatic enforcement: e.g., if uptime < 99.8%, funds automatically withheld and held in escrow for dispute resolution
automation_1.benefit: Removes need for constant manual monitoring, ensures objective enforcement, reduces disputes

automation_2.description: Real-time dashboards feeding from partner APIs, cloud monitoring, and financial systems. KPIs auto-calculated and flagged if below threshold. Alerts sent to relevant stakeholders.
automation_2.benefit: Early warning system, transparency, objective performance visibility

automation_3.description: Monthly automated compliance checks: Is partner still certified ISO 27001? Are they on OFAC sanctions list? Do they have required insurance? Automated reports generated.

automation_4.description: 90 days before NDA expiration, automated notification to both parties. Renewal terms pre-filled. E-signature workflow auto-triggered. New NDA effective immediately upon e-signature.
automation_4.benefit: No lapse in confidentiality protection, reduced administrative burden

automation_5.description: Monthly financial data feeds directly from partner systems into Sheikha financial system. Revenue calculated automatically, Zakat (2.5%) calculated, settled within 45 days. Blockchain receipt retained.
automation_5.benefit: Transparent, rapid payment, Islamic compliance automated, audit trail permanent
